Pay yourself first. Look, this is one of the basic building blocks. There's nobody going to tell you to pay yourself last. It just wouldn't make any sense, but you're doing that. Pay yourself first. Everybody agrees with it. Very few people do it. Now, how do you do it? First of all, let me show you how important it is. The IRS, the IRS gets their money first. You literally don't even have the option. They drag out of your paycheck first because they want to be paid. They don't trust you and they don't trust me. Now, I'm going to be talking about the code later. One of the things I did was I've spent a lot of time studying the code because I want to know everywhere I can go. But it doesn't matter. The code doesn't matter if you aren't paying yourself first. So this is what I do. When I get a paycheck, I can't help but go there. I'm going to maximize my exemptions for sure, no doubt. If they allow me 9, 10, 11, 50, I'm taking every one of them again. We'll be going over that in the future. But right now what I want to do is pay myself first. If $10,000 comes in, if $4,000 comes in, or if $1,000 comes in, I'm peeling away for a grant. I need to figure out some kind of way. If I'm getting a check from an employer or if money's coming to me, I need to figure out some kind of way to pay grant before you pay American Express, MasterCard, Visa, Rent, car payments. Why am I going to pay Toyota Motor Credit? I hadn't paid myself. Why am I going to pay the landlord I hadn't paid? Who do I love more than me? Nobody. Okay? I love me first. First me, then we're going to pay the wife, then we're going to pay the kids, then we're going to buy them clothes and food, and you know, see, I got to pay myself first. I can't take care of anybody if I can't take care of me first. So make it a priority to start paying yourself first. Do it now to start saying, hey, I get a check every month. Go to your employer. I get a check for $4,000. This is what I've been averaging, $2,000, whatever. I need some money set aside for me. The most important person in my life, I need some money set aside for me so that I can take care of the most important people in my life, which is probably your wife, your husband, the kids, your parents, all the other things you want to do, your future, your dreams. Pay yourself first and you will one day be in a position to start taking care of the people you love and the dreams you want to make happen.
